rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA BY AND THROUGH VS. CHOPPER CITY A CRIMINAL STREET GANG SUED AS AN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Modify Permanent Injunction Related To Chopper City, Eddy Rock, And Knock Out Posse (California Civil Code 3424, California Code Of Civil Procedure 533)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 7, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'sMotion To Modify Permanent Injunction Related To Chopper City, Eddy Rock, And Knock Out Posse (California Civil Code 3424, California Code Of Civil Procedure 533). Plaintiff People of the State of California's motion to modify the permanent injunction is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: EXPRESS MESSENGER SYSTEMS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Arbitration And For Stay Of Dlse Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 7, 2018, Line 3, PETITIONER EXPRESS MESSENGER SYSTEMS, INC., SUBCONTRACTING CONCEPTS (CT) LLC's Petition To Compel Arbitration And For Stay Of Dlse Proceedings. Petitioners Express Messenger Systems, Inc. and Subcontracting Concepts CT, LLC,'s petition to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is denied. The arbitration agreement is unconscionable and severance of the substantively unconscionable provisions is not possible because the entire agreement is permeated with unconscionability. The New York choice of law provision is unconscionable and California law applies. (Pinela v Neiman Marcus Group (2015) 238 Cal.App.4th 227, 246-247). The arbitration agreement is procedurally unconscionable. Subcontracting presented the adhesive contract in a take-it-or-leave-it manner and respondent Edvan Medeiros, whose English skills are limited, was asked to sign it "on the spot." (Medeiros Dec. pars. 1-7.) Paragraph 26 of the agreement refers to the American Arbitration Association, but the agreement does not clearly state what rules will govern the arbitration proceedings and Mr. Medeiros was never provided a copy of the governing rules. "The level of oppression is increased when, as here, the employer not only fails to provide a copy of the governing rules, but also fails to clearly identify which rules will govern so the employee could locate and review them." (Carbajal v. CWPSC, Inc. (2016) 245 Cal.App.4th 227, 245). The arbitration agreement is substantively unconscionable. The agreement requires the parties to share the costs of arbitration and such costs are often prohibitively expensive and pose a barrier for an alleged employee such as Mr. Medeiros to vindicate his legal rights. (Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83, 108, 110-111). The agreement not only precludes Mr. Medeiros from taking advantage of the relatively inexpensive remedy of a hearing before the Labor Commissioner, it also bars representative and private attorney general claims. The arbitration agreement also bars the recovery of punitive damages, statutory penalties, equitable relief, and attorney's fees, which deprives Mr. Medeiros of statutory remedies. The arbitration agreement is permeated with unconscionability and thus severance of the unconscionable terms is not possible. Subcontracting's request to stay the DLSE claim is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for respondent is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: MAURICE OCHOA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Request For Admissions, Set One Admitted And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 7, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT BNSF RAILWAY COMPANY Motion To Deem Request For Admissions, Set One Admitted And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion of defendant BNSF Railway Company for an order (1) deeming matters admitted and (2) for monetary sanctions is moot as to the former issue because facially compliant responses to the requests for admission were served before the hearing on the motion and granted as to the latter issue as to which sanctions are awarded in the amount of $400 in favor of BNSF and against plaintiff's attorney, provided that the Court will revisit the amount of sanctions in the event counsel to BNSF is required to attend an in-person hearing on this motion. Counsel to BNSF shall submit a proposed order in Word format consistent with the foregoing by email to jameshfleming@gmail.com prior to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HK)

rulings.Case Title: NANCI CLARENCE et al VS. COVE CONSTRUCTION, A PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against M.C. Plastering Co., Inc. On The Duty To Defend
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 7, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT COVE CONSTRUCTION, A PARTNERSHIP, MICHAEL TEAGUE, ROBERT CAIN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against M.C. Plastering Co., Inc. On The Duty To Defend. Cross-Complainants Cove Construction, Michael Teague, and Robert Cain's motion for summary adjudication against M.C. Plastering Co., Inc. on the issue of duty to defend is continued to June 14, 2018 to give the moving parties and all whom they contend owe them a duty to defend to work out an agreement "how such a defense should be provided or financed." (Crawford v. Weather Shield Mfg. Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541, 565, n.12.) . Per the indemnification provision of its contract with Cove and Civil Code 2778(4), M.C. has an ongoing duty to defend Cove since plaintiffs Nanci Clarence and Lidia Szajko have alleged that M.C.'s negligent stucco work caused in part the defects they are complaining about. (Crawford). However, because there appears to be several other subcontractors in a similar position to M.C. with regard to a duty to defend owed to Cove, footnote 12 in Crawford teaches that this court, before making a ruling on the timing and scope of M.C.'s duty to defend, should give the "affected parties" an opportunity to agree on "how such a defense should be provided or financed." As explained in footnote 12, when "an ongoing duty to defend is owed by multiple parties" under their respective indemnification agreements, the court has discretion to "permit the litigation to proceed with counsel chosen and paid by the party to whom the duty is owed, subject to a later determination of how damages for breach of the duty to defend should be apportioned among the breaching parties," but this discretion should only be exercised after the "parties, acting in good faith¿cannot agree on how such a defense should be provided or financed." (Crawford, n. 12.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: NIALL O'BRIEN VS. JOHN FINLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 7, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANT JOHN FINLEY, JANET FINLEY's Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution. Defendants John and Janet Finley's motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution is denied. Defendants did not provide 45 days' notice as required by CRC 3.1242(a). While the court does not and should not address the issue of whether the filing of the motion constitutes a general appearance, counsel for plaintiff Niall O'Brien is strongly encouraged to avoid motion practice on this issue by serving the defendants with summons and complaint as soon as possible. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. O'Brien is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: NETFORTRIS ACQUISTION CO., INC VS. LA QUINTA INN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, To Quash Service Of Summons And To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 7, 2018, Line 14, DEFENDANT KAMAL BHAKTA's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, To Quash Service Of Summons And To Dismiss. Defendant Kamal Bhakta's motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. The default entered against Mr. Bhakta on September 25, 2017 and the default judgment entered against him on November 14, 2017 are now vacated. Mr. Bhakta has shown surprise, mistake and excusable neglect to warrant the relief he seeks per the discretionary relief provisions of CCP 473(b). Arguably plaintiff NetFortris Acquisition Co, Inc. should not have proceeded with this case in any fashion after the court's oral ruling dismissing this case based on the mandatory forum selection clause. Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and NetFortis has not identified any legal prejudice it would suffer by the granting of the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Bhakta is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: WILSON TOLEDO VS. FREIGHT LINE EXPRESS TRUCKING, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Discovery Responses And Order Issuing Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 7, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT XINMIN WU, FREIGHT LINE EXPRESS TRUCKING Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Discovery Responses And Order Issuing Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion to compel discovery responses and for sanctions is granted. Plaintiff shall serve discovery responses without objection within 15 days of the date of order. Sanctions of $750.00 are awarded in favor of defendants' and against plaintiff and his attorney, payable within 15 days of the date of order, provided that the Court will revisit the amount of sanctions in the event counsel to BNSF is required to attend an in-person hearing on this motion. Defendants' counsel shall submit a proposed order in Word format consistent with the foregoing by email to jameshfleming@gmail.com prior to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HK)

rulings.Case Title: ** TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY -BURBANK ** AARON JUSTIS ET AL VS. MICHAEL HODGES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 7, 2018, Line 20, DEFENDANT MICHAEL HODGES MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE. Defendant Michael Hodges' motion for transfer of venue is granted per CCP 397(c). Although venue is proper in San Francisco, Mr. Hodges has shown that a transfer of the action to Los Angeles Superior Court, North Central District - Burbank, will promote the ends of justice. The ends of justice will be served by transferring this action to the Los Angeles Superior Court where it can be related to the already pending case between the parties before that court. Having two distinct cases involving substantially the same parties arising out of the same core facts pending approximately 400 miles from each other seriously disserves judicial economy. Plaintiffs to pay all transfer costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Hodges is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: SHAWN P. EVANS ET AL VS. AVANDE INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Proceedings Pending Resolution Of Delaware Case; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 7, 2018, Line 21, DEFENDANT AVANDE, INC's. Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Proceedings Pending Resolution Of Delaware Case. Defendant Avande, Inc.'s motion to stay proceedings pending resolution of the action pending in the Court of Chancery in the state of Delaware is granted. Per this court's authority to stay a later filed action in favor of a related previously filed action, this case is stayed pending the completion of the Delaware case or further order of this court. Judicial economy is disserved by the concurrent prosecution of two lawsuits involving the same parties seeking damages against each other in two different fora approximately 3000 miles apart. There is no persuasive reason why the claims alleged by plaintiffs Shawn and Judith Evans in this case should not be re-filed and prosecuted as cross-claims in the Delaware lawsuit. Since both the amended complaint in the Delaware lawsuit and the first amended complaint filed in this court seek monetary relief, the ultimate damages issues are who, as between Mr. Evans and Avande, owes money to the other and how much. Instead of resolving these issues piecemeal with the possibility of inconsistent rulings, the far better course is to have them resolved in a single case. Since this court has no authority to dismiss or stay the Delaware lawsuit, the only way this court can avoid the unnecessary expense and complications of two different lawsuits is to stay or dismiss this case. A stay is preferable to a dismissal so that, in the event that the Delaware court no longer is able or willing to adjudicate the parties' disputes, this case can be revived without the need for the filing of a new case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Avande is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: IVY GROWTH HOLDINGS, LLC. VS. BELIEVE WIRELESS, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 7, 2018, Line 22, DEFENDANT BELIEVE WIRELESS, LLC., PAUL DOWLING's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Defendants Believe Wireless, LLC's and Paul Dowling's motion to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction is granted. Plaintiff Ivy Growth Holdings, LLC has not shown that defendants directed their activities at California residents or purposefully availed themselves of the benefits of the California. (Vons Companies, Inc. v. Seabest Foods, Inc. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 434, 446). Believe Wireless does not advertise in California, does not provide services in California, does not maintain an office in California and is not registered to do business in California. The fact that defendants entered into a contract with a California resident company does not establish that defendants have sufficient minimum contacts with California. The court is required to examine "prior negotiations and contemplated future consequences, along with the terms of the contract and the parties' actual course of dealing." (Burger King Corp. v. Rudzewicz (1985) 471 U.S. 462, 478 - 479). Ivy reached out to defendants in Maryland with an offer for a business transaction and the evidence on this motion shows that negotiations occurred via email between the parties. The contract does not envisage that Believe Wireless or Ivy perform any services in California. The contract provides that, in exchange for acquiring plaintiff's Maryland client, Believe Wireless would pay Ivy a monthly sum as long as it continues to provide services to that client in Maryland. Defendants' only conduct connected to California, besides contracting with a California entity, is the monthly payment required by the contract. The circumstances surrounding the contract do not show that defendants had the requisite minimum contacts with California to justify this court's exercise of personal jurisdiction over them. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)
